title: Ambiguous chatroom parsing: 16-char hex team names parsed as direct message IID
description:
File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Lines: 832-837

The XML parser uses a heuristic to detect direct messages: if chatroom is exactly 16 hex characters, treat it as an IID (8 bytes):
```python
if len(chat_group) == 16:
    try:
        dest_iid = bytes.fromhex(chat_group)
        dest_type = DestType.DIRECT
    except ValueError:
        pass
```

Problem: A team name that happens to be 16 hex characters (e.g., 'a1b2c3d4e5f67890' used as a custom chatroom name) would be incorrectly parsed as a direct message IID.

Impact:
1. Message routing broken - sent to wrong recipient
2. Silent data corruption in roundtrip
3. Potential security issue if messages are misdirected

Fix: The protocol should use explicit markers. Short term, check if it matches a known team name first:
```python
# Check team names first before falling back to IID parsing
team_name = chat_group.lower()
if team_name.startswith('team '):
    team_name = team_name[5:]
team = TEAM_BY_NAME.get(team_name)
if team:
    dest_type = DestType.TEAM
    dest_team = team
elif len(chat_group) == 16:
    # Only try IID parsing if not a team
    ...
```
